text:
The were an underground anti-establishment group of Eldians of Liberio in the 830s. Hailing from its internment zone, they plotted to destroy Marley from within using the Founding Titan, in order to restore Eldia as a world power and give to its people the freedom and dignity that they yearned. 

A radical group whose ideology was rooted in revanchism and imperialistic nostalgia, they negated the crimes attributed to the Eldian Empire, rejected that the world's portrayal of Subjects of Ymir as "spawn of the devil" by considering themselves the "chosen children of God", and desired to enact a violent revenge upon the Marleyan people.

Structure and organization

The Eldian Restorationists were created by Eren Kruger, an Eldian who managed to infiltratre the Marleyan Public Security forces. Known by the members of the group as "the Owl", he gave them weapons, funds, and historical documents under Marleyan posession, thus leading the anti-establishment group from the shadows. When Grisha Jaeger was recruited, due to his severe grudge towards Marley and his profession as a doctor, he quickly became the leading figure of the Restorationists.

The Restorationists tended to organize secret meetings in a basement somewhere in the internment zone, being disguised as a social dance club, whenever they received new information from Kruger, or when the Marleyan government made moves which could impact their goals.

The symbol of the group was a cross-shaped scar on each member's body, which they made themselves with a knife in order to officialize their membership. The Restorationists were heavy on comradeship, calling each other "brothers and sisters", "comrades", or "fellow patriots", although their use of these terms also applied to other Eldians who were not part of the group.

Ideology

 praising the Founder Ymir Fritz]]
The Eldian Restorationists believed themselves to be "enlightened" compared to the others Eldians in Liberio, negating Marleyan propaganda thought at school, which consisted into categorizing the Subjects of Ymir as a "filthy" and demonic race because of their ability to transform into Titans and the innumerable atrocities supposedly enacted by the Eldian Empire upon Marley and the rest of the world for millennia.

They held the Fritz family that fled to Paradis Island in contempt, considering them to be cowards and traitors due to having abandoned their ancestors in the mainland without protection from the hatred of the other nations and peoples. The Restorationists believed that they needed to be punished, by having the Founding Titan taken away from them and give it to the descendants of the royal line who decided to stay in the continental mainland in order to rule over the Eldian people if they managed to defeat Marley and obtain freedom.

Anti-Marleyan sentiment

Because of the daily oppression they suffered at the hands of Marleyan authorities and public, the members of the group held a fanatical hatred towards the nation of Marley, and vowed to take revenge for all these years of oppression. They believed that their ancestors were in the right, and that the only way to right the wrongs endured by the Eldians on the mainland was to bring Eldia back to power and be ruthless to its ennemies. They believed the world to be rotten, their era to be the "Dark Ages", and only with Eldia being back on top would the world be corrected.

Historical revisionism

With the information and history books provided to them by the Owl, they developped a warped history of the Eldian Empire and Ymir Fritz. The documents were written in a barely intelligible language, but looking at illustrations, they concluded that Ymir Fritz had used the power of the Titans to cultivate lands, build roads, and bring wealth to mankind. They thus believed that there was not any atrocity committed upon non-Eldians, and that the empire only brought peace and happiness to the different peoples of the world, because Eldians never had such a brutal culture since the Ymir would have never allowed the kind of atrocities Marley claims the Eldian Empire to have committed. 

Racial supremacy

The Eldian Restorationists also believed Subjects of Ymir to be the "chosen children of God", thus adhering to the racial supremacist mindset at the time of the empire which consisted in the belief that Subjects of Ymir were a superior race above all others thanks to their Titan powers and imperial dominance over the whole world. They took pride in their ethnic and racial identity, as a means to defy the Marleyan narratives surrounding the Eldian people.

History

The remnants of the royal family that stayed in Marley formed a revolutionary army; however, this army was unsuccessful and was crushed. All members of this original organization, including Eren Kruger's father, were smoked out by Marleyan Public Security and executed for treason against Marley.

Grisha Jaeger joined the Restorationists at the age of 18 when a member of the organization came to his clinic and told him that they received information that his sister had been killed by a man in the Marley authorities. Grisha devoted himself to taking down Marley and reviving Eldia. As he was interpreting an ancient scroll sent by the Owl, he met Dina Fritz, a woman of royal blood whose ancestors decided not to move to Paradis Island. The two married and had a son, Zeke Jaeger. Grisha was sure Zeke would lead them to victory over Marley. When the Warrior program of the Marley government was proclaimed, he planned to make Zeke a Warrior who was then supposed to turn against Marley. However, at the age of 7, Zeke reported his parents to the authorities to protect himself and his grandparents, and the Restorationists were arrested. They were then sentenced to be sent to Paradis Island, where they would roam as Pure Titans as they feed on other Eldians.

Before being sent to Paradis Island, Grisha was tortured by the authorities for information such as the Restorationists plans, and the identity of "the Owl." After this, the organization members were shipped to Paradis Island in preparation of their execution. All members of the group were transformed into Pure Titans, except Grice and Grisha. The former, who was simply kicked off the wall to be eaten by the Titans, and the latter, who was kept alive by Kruger for further questioning. Once most of the public service authorities had returned to their ship, Kruger saved Grisha from execution by killing Gross. He then revealed himself as "the Owl," transformed into a Titan, and begun slaughtering the other authorities.

After returning to Grisha from his massacre, Kruger explained his reasoning for saving Grisha and gave him a mission to restore Eldia's freedom and dignity by entering the Walls to retake the Founding Titan. He explained that Grisha would do this by using the Titan powers he would give to him. At first, Kruger was met with resistance from a burnt out Grisha, but after giving him a picture of his family and convincing him further, Grisha's will was reignited and he accepted Kruger's mission.

Trivia

Despite being spoken before "Restorationists" in Episode 57, the official English subtitles ignore it and translate the name of the organization as just the "Restorationists." However, in Episode 63, the subtitles include "Eldia".
